danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct themed environments (magnetic fields, spark effects, shadow areas, needle obstacles)
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats; haywire Robot Masters controlling mining worlds

danger_signals.visual_form: Masked adversary sprite matching Mega Man's visual complexity; appears at stage transitions
danger_signals.meaning: Mini-boss threat; mysterious opposing force with comparable combat capabilities

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage-specific enemy types firing colored projectiles; increased density in later stages
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger; Robot Masters' influence over local environments

danger_signals.visual_form: Darker, more industrial stage design; mechanical hazards and trap patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Dr. Wily's return to villainy; final conflict zone after betrayal

danger_signals.visual_form: Mega Man sprite potentially flashing or visual damage indicators (standard NES convention)

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprites (health energy, weapon ammunition, E-Tanks with distinct visual markers)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; stage completion rewards; player progression security

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage end transition; Dr. Light's lab return state (implied safe zone)
safety_signals.meaning: Mission progress; temporary safety from active threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Canine sidekick sprite; transformation states (Coil, Jet, Marine)
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage unlocked; expanded movement/evasion capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible 1-UP items with distinctive sprite design

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Man blue color scheme; canine companion Rush (suggests technology/control)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dr. Light's lab (safe zone); peaceful world state before robot corruption

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Eight Robot Masters with distinct thematic designs (Top Man, Shadow Man, Spark Man, Magnet Man, Hard Man, Snake Man, Gemini Man, Needle Man); fortress architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Doc Robots in later stages possess previous Mega Man enemies' abilities—visual remixing of known threats
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mining worlds (haywire state); Wily's fortress (dark, industrial); stage-specific hazard theming tied to robot master weapon types

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mask; comparable sprite complexity to Mega Man; revealed as Mega Man's older brother
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mini-boss encounters throughout stages; visual parity with protagonist suggests peer relationship
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage transition points; appears at critical narrative junctures; eventually saves Mega Man during fortress collapse

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Eight distinct Robot Master designs; each with unique stage aesthetic and weapon signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Same designs reappear as Doc Robots in later challenge stages (visual continuity with thematic variation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mining worlds; each stage reflects robot master's theme (magnetism, hardness, spinning tops, shadows, sparks, snakes, gemini duality, needles)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Safe lab environment; Dr. Light and Dr. Wily cooperation implied; Gamma robot under construction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Calm stage transitions; no active conflict until robot master corruption event

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Robot Masters go haywire / Mining worlds corrupted
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight distinct themed environments become active danger zones; stage-specific visual corruption (magnetic distortions, spark fields, shadow overtakes, needle hazards)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies spawn and attack; Robot Masters control territory; Mega Man must systematically clear zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crystal-based objectives implied in stage design; stages functionally represent mining/collection sites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robot Master bosses serve as guardians; defeating each yields crystal recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress appears; darker, more industrial visual palette; stage design shifts from mining world themes to mechanical hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Doc Robots appear with remix abilities; final confrontation state activated; fortress destruction sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Collapsing architecture; red danger coloration (implied); Break Man/Proto Man rescue appears at narrative climax
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final Wily boss fight; Proto Man saves Mega Man; Wily crushed under rubble; crisis resolution

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage thematic design linked to Robot Master identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Top Man's stage features spinning platforms and rotational hazards; Shadow Man's stage uses darkness/shadow mechanics; Spark Man's stage incorporates electrical visual effects and conductivity mechanics. Weapon weakness chains (Robot Master A's weapon destroys Robot Master B's stage defenses) reinforce mechanical hierarchy without explicit narrative exposition.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Mining world motif for resource collection
environmental_storytelling.example: Eight stages contextualized as mining sites containing Gamma power crystals. Stage architecture uses mining/extraction visual language (crystalline formations, excavation equipment, resource nodes) to justify boss encounters and reward collection mechanics.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Fortress architecture as climactic world-state shift
environmental_storytelling.example: Transition from colorful, thematic mining worlds to dark, industrial fortress signals Dr. Wily's return to villainy and narrative escalation. Visual shift from environment-specific hazards to mechanical/trap-focused design implies increasing threat severity.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Break Man/Proto Man recurring visual presence
environmental_storytelling.example: Masked figure appears at stage transition points before identification. Consistent sprite design across encounters creates visual continuity and mystery. Final reveal as Mega Man's brother recontextualizes all previous encounters as protective intervention.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Rush transformation unlocks as progression reward
environmental_storytelling.example: Canine companion's visual forms (Coil, Jet, Marine) unlock after stage completion. New mobility options visually manifest in subsequent stages, allowing access to previously blocked areas. Environmental design expands as new Rush forms become available, showing world-state responsiveness to player capability.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard run, jump, and shoot; limited mobility; vulnerable to stage hazards
Access to Robot Master's weapon; can now exploit weakness chains; new tactical options available
Enhanced mobility options (higher jumps via Coil, flight via Jet, underwater access via Marine); stage design opens new areas based on Rush transformation availability
Occupy stage territory; spawn themed enemies; deploy signature weapons; follow predictable attack patterns with stage-specific visual telegraphing
Appears at stage transitions as mini-boss; contests Mega Man but doesn't pursue lethal outcomes; visual parity suggests evenly-matched conflict
Saves Mega Man during fortress collapse; shifts from adversarial to protective positioning; narrative alignment clarified by action
Controls Doc Robots; summons escalating challenges; final boss encounter shows direct confrontation; crushed under rubble at fortress collapse, visually ending threat
Possess remixed abilities from previous Mega Man games' enemies; appear in challenge stages between Robot Masters and final boss; visual similarity to known threats with behavioral variation

Thematic spawning patterns matching stage identity (magnetic enemies in Magnet Man's stage, spark-type enemies in Spark Man's stage, etc.); density and aggression increase in later stages and fortress zones

notes: Mega Man 3 uses stark environmental theming and Robot Master identity to communicate world-state shifts. The game's faction structure is implicit: Dr. Light (helper), Mega Man (protagonist agent), Dr. Wily (hidden antagonist revealed mid-game), and Proto Man (mysterious third faction resolved as protective ally). Danger is signaled through enemy density, stage visual darkness/complexity, and hazard theming. Safety signals manifest through power-up availability, companion unlocks, and stage completion progression. The password system allows state persistence, though sources don't detail visual indicators for this mechanic. Boss weakness chains (documented in gameplay section) create emergent visual/strategic storytelling where stage order selection reveals player understanding of faction hierarchy. The eight-stage structure followed by four Doc Robot challenge stages, then Wily fortress, creates a three-act visual and mechanical escalation pattern.
